description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a ribbed texture, likely intended to provide traction or drainage. The block is solid and has a dark, mottled stone appearance, suggesting it is made from a dense material such as granite or basalt. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the textured top. Its overall form is geometric and precise, indicating it was manufactured or cut for a specific functional purpose. This type of block is commonly used in urban environments as a barrier, step, or part of a drainage system.
description: A dark, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, giving it a ribbed and textured appearance. The sides are smooth and the block appears dense and heavy.
description_view_0: The block is seen from the grooved side, with the parallel grooves appearing as thin, dark lines across the top surface.
description_view_1: The grooves are more pronounced and appear as deep channels running from front to back, emphasizing the ribbed texture.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, the grooves are visible as horizontal lines, but the lighting and angle make the surface texture more apparent.
description_view_3: The grooves are again prominent, running vertically in this view, highlighting the depth and spacing between each channel.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or street as a barrier or drainage cover
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: set flush with adjacent pavement or stone blocks
